description_long: This object consists of a cluster of six cylindrical canisters arranged in a hexagonal pattern, all held together by a metal frame. The canisters are matte olive green, with faint stenciled markings visible on their sides, suggesting military or industrial use. A single, sturdy handle extends from the base of the cluster, wrapped in a textured material for grip. The overall construction appears robust, with metal bands securing the canisters in place. The object has a utilitarian, rugged appearance, with no decorative elements. Its design suggests it is intended for manual deployment or placement.
description: A cluster of six olive green cylindrical canisters secured by a metal frame, with a single wrapped handle for carrying.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the hexagonal arrangement of six canisters and the central handle extending downward.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the cylindrical shape of the canisters and the length of the handle.
description_view_2: Another side view, showing the canisters' stenciled markings and the handle's attachment point.
description_view_3: Angled view displaying both the canister cluster and the handle, emphasizing the object's compact, grouped structure.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: The object appears to have a detonation cap or fuse at the top of the canister cluster, likely activated by manual or timed means.
